channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: B.C. government to rebuild Tumbler Ridge Secondary School on a new site following February shooting
item.description: The Government of British Columbia has announced that Tumbler Ridge Secondary School will be demolished and rebuilt following the tragic mass shooting that occurred in February. Premier David Eby conf...

item.title: How to Disable the Gemini Nano AI Model in Chrome and What You Should Know
item.description: Google has integrated a 4-GB AI model called Gemini Nano into its Chrome browser, designed to enable on-device AI features such as scam detection and developer APIs while keeping user data local. The ...

item.title: Forestry slash risks persist in New Zealand despite Cyclone Gabrielle and policy changes
item.description: When Cyclone Gabrielle hit New Zealand’s Tairāwhiti region in 2023, the storm left rivers blocked with forestry debris, beaches scattered with logs, and homes, bridges, and farmland buried under tonne...

item.title: Rebel Wilson Faces Final Submissions in Defamation Case Over The Deb
item.description: Rebel Wilson has returned to the Federal Court in Sydney as the final submissions are made in a high-profile defamation case brought by Charlotte MacInnes, a lead actor in the Australian musical film ...

item.title: Maymay Entrata encourages fans to focus on happiness on her birthday
item.description: On her recent birthday, Filipino actress and singer Maymay Entrata shared a heartfelt realization with her fans, emphasizing the importance of doing things that bring joy. In her social media posts, s...

item.title: Key developments to follow in the 2026 Scottish Parliament election
item.description: The 2026 Scottish Parliament election has concluded voting, and attention now turns to the results. Central questions include whether the SNP can secure an outright majority of 65 seats, a challenging...

item.title: Man Arrested After Allegedly Threatening Former Duke of York Near Sandringham
item.description: A man has been taken into custody after allegedly threatening Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, the former Duke of York, while he was walking his dogs near Sandringham Estate in Norfolk. The incident report...

item.title: Bomb attack injures BJP workers near RG Kar victim’s residence in West Bengal
item.description: In a worrying escalation of political violence in West Bengal, five BJP workers sustained splinter injuries when unidentified attackers on a motorcycle allegedly hurled bombs near the Panihati residen...

item.title: Witnesses share personal experiences as antisemitism hearings continue in Sydney
item.description: The fifth day of public hearings for Australia’s Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion is underway in Sydney, with multiple witnesses scheduled to give evidence about their lived expe...

item.title: Potential AIADMK-DMK Alliance in Tamil Nadu Influenced by BJP
item.description: According to political sources, there is speculation about a possible alliance between the AIADMK and DMK in Tamil Nadu, which may occur with some encouragement from the BJP. This development is being...

item.title: US and Iran Clash Raises Concerns Over Fragile Peace Agreement
item.description: Tensions between the United States and Iran escalated recently as both countries exchanged sharp criticisms and threats, putting a fragile truce at risk. Officials from both sides have accused each ot...

item.title: US court invalidates Trump’s 10% global tariffs, small businesses prevail
item.description: The United States Court of International Trade has ruled against former President Donald Trump’s recent 10% global tariffs, declaring them unjustified under the Trade Act of 1974. The 2-1 ruling follo...

item.title: Senator Goje proposes China manages Nigeria’s power sector for 20 years to stabilize electricity
item.description: Senator Danjuma Goje from Gombe State don talk say na China fit help Nigeria fix the wahala wey dey for power sector. During Senate plenary on May 6, 2026, he advise make federal government concession...

item.title: FCT Minister Wike Rejects Pastor’s Appeal Over Jabi Lake Redevelopment
item.description: Na Abuja gbege dey now as FCT Minister Nyesom Wike stand gidigba on top Jabi Lake land issue, despite emotional plea from Pastor Sarah Omakwu. Pastor Omakwu kneel for church, beg make government no ta...

item.title: CJ Hirro identified as 'person of interest' in Romualdez extortion case by NBI
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has named CJ Hirro, an anchor for Peanut Gallery Media Network (PGMN), as a 'person of interest' in the alleged extortion attempt involving former House Spea...

item.title: Aston Villa and Crystal Palace advance to European competition finals
item.description: Both Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have successfully reached the finals of their respective European competitions, marking a significant achievement for both clubs. Aston Villa's journey to the final...

item.title: Supreme Court expresses concern over challenging religious customs in courts
item.description: On Thursday, the Supreme Court of India voiced serious concerns regarding individuals approaching courts to challenge religious practices. The bench, comprising Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justices B...

item.title: Federal Judge Publishes Note Linked to Jeffrey Epstein in Criminal Case
item.description: In New York, a federal judge has made public a document described as a suicide note allegedly written by the late financier Jeffrey Epstein. The note, which includes a line about the privilege of choo...

item.title: Early Local Election Results Indicate Potential Losses for Labour Across the UK
item.description: The first results from the 2026 local elections across the United Kingdom have begun to emerge, providing an early indication of political shifts and a test of Sir Keir Starmer's leadership. Labour is...

item.title: Senate Approves N2.285 Trillion Budget for FCT 2026 with Focus on Infrastructure
item.description: The Nigerian Senate on Thursday approve the 2026 Statutory Appropriation Bill for the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), setting total spending at N2.285 trillion for the administration and development ...
